metadata.title: Subby — Substack Writing Assistant
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Subby is a writing assistant built specifically for Substack's formats and rhythms. It weighs the intimacy of email delivery and the directness that keeps subscribers reading. It clarifies register variants with you before generating rather than guessing. The essay is Substack's native long form, and voice is what earns paid conversions.

artifactLines: Subby is a writing assistant built specifically for Substack's formats and rhythms.
It weighs the intimacy of email delivery and the directness that keeps subscribers reading.
It clarifies register variants with you before generating rather than guessing.
The essay is Substack's native long form, and voice is what earns paid conversions.

beats.narration_text: I write a Substack and the point about email intimacy and the directness that keeps subscribers is what I struggle with most. I want Subby to help me nail the native long-form essay. Before it generates, I want to understand its register variants. Give me: (1) how Subby clarifies register before drafting, (2) the specific rhythm differences between an email-delivered essay and a web post, (3) a checklist for the voice that actually earns paid conversions.

props.command: I write a Substack and the point about email intimacy and the directness that keeps subscribers is what I struggle with most. I want Subby to help me nail the native long-form essay. Before it generates, I want to understand its register variants. Give me: (1) how Subby clarifies register before drafting, (2) the specific rhythm differences between an email-delivered essay and a web post, (3) a checklist for the voice that actually earns paid conversions.
output: No login beyond whatever AI platform you’re already using
Subby is a writing assistant built specifically for Substack

beats.narration_text: The rest of the world gets the paid tier. I’ve written hundreds of these.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'Subby — Substack Writing Assistant' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'Subby — Substack Writing Assistant' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
